rationale: The citing paper uses Rocklage and Fazio (2020) as more than background: it is repeatedly cited as evidence that emotional language can have backfiring effects, not just benefits. The paper also summarizes this study in its core literature table and explicitly relies on its hedonic-versus-utilitarian finding to motivate H5 about when emotionality should be less detrimental. This indicates a meaningful role in the citing paper's conceptual framing and hypothesis development.
relation_description: The citing paper extends Rocklage and Fazio's findings on emotional language effects in consumer reviews to brand-generated social media communication and brand status inferences. It carries forward the idea of potential backfire and product-type contingency into a new outcome domain and mechanism framework.
